name: Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the ingredients in a large bowl.
2. Let it ferment for five days, stirring daily.
3. The starter can be kept indefinitely in the refrigerator. However, it's best to use it regularly and replenish the starter by discarding half of it and adding fresh ingredients.
4. If the liquid rises to the top during storage, just stir it back in.
Note: Many sourdough recipes use a sponge method of rising. Mix about 1 cup of starter with every 6 cups of flour and 4-6 cups of water to make the sponge. Ferment this mixture overnight at room temperature. Then replace the starter used from the new mixture, add oil, salt, and enough flour to make the dough. Knead, shape into loaves, allow to rise for two hours, then bake (425°F for 20 minutes, reduce heat to 375°F and bake another 1-1/4 hours, brushing loaves with water before each oven entry).
For English muffins:
1. Instead of shaping into loaves, roll the dough out to about 1/4 inch thick and cut into rounds.
2. Let rise for an hour or so until doubled in size.
3. Bake slowly on an ungreased griddle, starting with high heat and reducing heat to brown slowly for each batch (about 5-6 minutes per side). Rolling the dough out on cornmeal gives them a nice finish.
4. The muffins freeze well, but you may want to fork split them first before freezing.
